titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Robert B. Stepto Papers
titlestmt.author: by Robert Bartels

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday June 24, 2026 at 23:01

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection documents the professional career of Robert B. Stepto, professor of African American Studies, American Studies, and English at Yale University.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Boxes 19-21 (student records): Restricted until 2098. For further information consult the appropriate curator.
Box 22 (administrative records): Restricted until 2058. For further information consult the appropriate curator.
Box 23 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Boxes 24-25: Mold was present throughout boxes upon receipt, but is no longer active. Use caution when handling.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Robert B. Stepto (born 1945) is a professor of African American Studies, American Studies, and English at Yale. He graduated from Trinity College in 1966 and received MA and PhD degrees in English from Stanford University. He taught at Williams College for three years before joining the faculty at Yale University in 1974. From 1978-1981 Stepto was the first director of graduate studies in the African American studies department. Stepto's primary research areas are African American autobiography, fiction, poetry and visual arts since 1840. He is the author of several books, including From Behind the Veil: A Study of Afro-American Narrative (1979), Blue As the Lake: A Personal Geography (1998), and A Home Elsewhere: Reading African American Classics in the Age of Obama (2010). He is currently the John M. Schiff Professor of English Emeritus.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection documents the professional career of Robert B. Stepto, professor of African American Studies, American Studies, and English at Yale University. Materials include research and subject files relating to Stepto's scholarly work in the fields of African American Studies and English Literature, and teaching files relating to Stepto's academic career at Williams College and Yale University. Also included are manuscript drafts and journal articles written by Stepto, as well as professional correspondence with colleagues at Yale and the wider African American studies profession. Materials include research files and correspodence with notable American Americans authors, scholars, and poets, including Toni Morrison, Michael S. Harper, Robert Hayden, and Sterling A. Brown.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is arranged into four series: Research and subject files; Teaching and Yale files; Writings; and Correspondence.

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Some printed material authored by Robert Stepto was removed from the collection during accessioning for separate cataloging and can be accessed by searching the library's online catalog.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Robert B. Stepto Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Audiovisual material. Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: Inquiry for the Eighties: A Survey of African-American Literary Thought
National Endowment for the Humanities research grant

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Note on original case reads, "Prof. Stepto: At around H9971 the Moyers interview with Richard Rodriguez begins. I taped as much as I could. It aired during the last week in April. You may want to use the phone and address that Moyers shows at the end of his programs if you would like to order the full interview. Thank you for everything, Felice".

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Audiovisual material. Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of published articles and journals, and photocopies of articles and book chapters covering topics relating to African American studies and English literature. Some contain handwritten notes and comments by Stepto.

did.unittitle: "After Modernism, After Hibernation: Michael Harper, Robert Hayden, and Jay Wright"
"After the 1960's: The Boom in Afro-American Fiction"

title.part: Chant of Saints: A Gathering of Afro-American Literature, Art, and Scholarship

did.unittitle: The Death and Life of Malcolm X , review
From Behind the Veil: Afro-American Reform Literature at the Turn of the Century , PhD thesis
Harper, Michael S., writings and remarks
Narrative of the Life of Frederick Douglass, an American Slave , introduction
Remarks delivered in sessions at the American Studies Association Convention and the Modern Language Association Convention
Report on "Ralph Ellison: The Genesis of an Artist"
Reviews of books by Robert B. Stepto
"Storytelling in Early Afro-American Fiction: Frederick Douglass's The Heroic Slave "
"Three Questions Concerning Richard Wright and the Afro-American Literary Tradition"
"When de Saints go Ma'chin' Home: Sterling Brown's Blueprint for a New Negro Poetry"
Write Me a Tale: Storytelling in the Afro-American Narrative , drafts
Callaloo, A Journal of African Diaspora Arts and Letters , publishing correspondence
Singh, Amritjit, Eduardo Gonzales, and Michael Harper
